summary: The tooth begins its eruption phase around six months old as per early developmental timelines discussed by dental literature. According to Nolla’s staging system, this corresponds closely to his Stage 6 where significant movement towards surface is noted but not yet complete. Agent1 reasoned based on established medical knowledge regarding infant dentition timing and specific definitions provided within Nollas' classification framework related directly to when a tooth initiates moving into position from below gum level until fully visible or erupted through skin/gum tissue. This summary captures both critical aspects asked—age range initiation & precise nomenclature used during initial phases leading up to total exposure via gums/epidermis layer post-birth.
